<!-- generated by `make bench-docs` (scripts/bench_report.py --page); regenerate on a representative machine, don't hand-edit -->
# Benchmarks

Representative single-machine numbers for **v0.21.0**, committed by hand â€” *not* from CI (shared runners aren't hardware-representative).

Two builds are shown so you can see the from-source upside:

- **portable** â€” `-O3 -march=x86-64-v2 -ffast-math` â€” the shipped PyPI wheel.
- **native** â€” `-O3 -march=native -mprefer-vector-width=256 -ffast-math` â€” `-DDOPPLER_NATIVE=ON`, built from source for this CPU.

**Environment** â€” measured 2026-06-25 08:42:01 UTC, doppler `1542287`, cc (GCC) 16.1.1 20260430.

- CPU: **AMD Ryzen AI 9 465 w/ Radeon 880M** â€” 20 threads, governor `performance`, boost on
- OS: 7.1.1-2-cachyos, glibc 2.43
- Libs: Python 3.13.13, NumPy 2.4.5, CMake 4.3.4

Throughput is **MSa/s** (higher is better); latency ops are mean **time/call** (lower is better). *from src* = native vs portable.

> The two builds are measured **interleaved** (alternating runs, per-benchmark best), so *from src* reflects the real build difference. Big gains are vectorizable kernels under AVX-512; overhead-bound benches (tiny per-call work) sit near 0% because the build can't help where Python-call overhead dominates.

## Transport (P0)

Wire throughput and control-plane latency for the two transports the `stream` module ships, measured by the `bench_stream` C harness (block = 65536 CF32 samples, 600 frames, best of 3 passes) over TCP loopback.

- **ZMQ** â€” brokerless PUSH/PULL, the streaming data plane.
- **NATS** â€” the durable JetStream work-queue tier (server-acked publish + explicit-ack pull). Its lower throughput is the price of exactly-once durability, not overhead.

| metric | ZMQ | NATS (JetStream) |
| --- | ---: | ---: |
| firehose throughput â€” MSa/s | 945.7 | 61.1 |
| firehose throughput â€” MB/s | 7,215 | 466.3 |
| status-plane RTT mean â€” Âµs | 19.3 | 35.5 |
| status-plane RTT p99 â€” Âµs | 30.0 | 43.0 |

> JetStream is store-and-forward, so the harness one-way *firehose* latency degenerates to queue residency there and is omitted; the status-plane REQ/REP RTT is the comparable latency figure. Captured with `make bench-stream` (a broker is started and torn down automatically).

## Release history (portable build)

Portable-build throughput across releases â€” the wheel numbers. Comparable only across releases measured on the same machine.
